plan: Sell all leveraged ETFs (SOXL, SPAL) to eliminate high risk.
Trim SMH, SPY, and CRWD to reduce concentration.
Add QQQI to increase income holdings.
Buy XLF to add a diversified sector.
This plan prioritizes capital preservation with a significant cash buffer.
Expect lower volatility and modest growth.
conservative.read: This mode targets ~1.5% monthly growth with a focus on capital preservation. It significantly reduces leverage and concentration risk, aiming for lower drawdowns.

plan: Trim SOXL and sell SPAL to reduce leverage.
Trim SMH and SPY to lower concentration.
Add CRWD, XLF, and MSFT to diversify and strengthen holdings.
This plan balances growth with risk reduction.
It aims for steady growth with moderate drawdowns.
Cash buffer will be around 10%.
balanced.read: This mode targets ~2.5% monthly growth with a risk-first approach. It balances broad market exposure, quality tech, and limited leverage, aiming for consistent growth with controlled drawdowns.

plan: Increase SOXL and SMH exposure to capitalize on potential upside.
Add TECS and PANW, strong scanner setups, for potential rapid gains.
Trim SPY slightly to manage concentration.
This plan aggressively pursues growth through leverage and concentration.
Expect higher potential returns but also significantly larger drawdowns.
Strict stop discipline is critical due to leverage and decay.
aggressive.read: This mode targets ~4% monthly growth by leaning into leverage and concentration. It embraces higher potential returns but comes with substantially increased risk, including larger drawdowns and the effects of leveraged ETF decay.

plan: Sell all leveraged ETFs and volatile single names (SOXL, SPAL, QQQ, CRWD, SMH).
Maximize allocation to QQQI for steady income generation.
Increase SPY for broad market exposure and some income potential.
Add XLF to diversify and capture potential income.
This plan prioritizes reliable monthly distributions and low drawdowns.
Expect modest NAV growth and consistent income payments.
income.read: This mode targets ~1% monthly growth by focusing on income generation and capital preservation. It rotates out of volatile assets into income-focused ETFs and broad index funds, aiming for steady distributions and low drawdowns.
